channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Agent Memory Providers Compared — Honcho, Mem0, Hindsight, and Five More
item.description: Modern assistants still forget everything when you close the tab unless something persists beyond the context window. Agent memory providers are services or libraries that hold facts and summaries...

item.title: Hermes Agent Memory System: How Persistent AI Memory Actually Works
item.description: You know the drill. You open a chat with an AI agent, explain your project, share your preferences, get some work done, and close the tab. Come back the following week and it's like talking to a...

item.title: Docker Microservices Demo with Cross-Language Containers
item.description: Docker Microservices Demo with Cross-Language Containers From Local Development to Docker - A Cross-Language Technical Roadmap By the end of this guide, you'll understand how to: Build a...

item.title: Build a Tip Calculator in Vanilla JavaScript: a beginner DOM project
item.description: A tip calculator is a great first DOM project. It requires user input, real-time calculation, and clean output — but no API calls, no data storage, no complexity you don't control. By the end of...

item.title: How to Merge and Concatenate Videos with FFmpeg
item.description: Originally published at ffmpeg-micro.com You need to join two or more video files into one. Maybe you're stitching together clips from a multi-camera shoot, assembling a highlight reel, or...

item.title: 5 Angular Material features that never make it into "top features" lists
item.description: Every "top Angular Material features" post lists the same six things: buttons, cards, datepicker, data table, theming, animations. Cool, we've all seen it. But Angular's still pulling around 3.8M...

item.title: Hot Take: You Should Ditch Next.js 15 for Svelte 5 – 50% Smaller Bundles
item.description: After migrating 14 production apps from Next.js 15 to Svelte 5 over the past 8 months, my team recorded a median 52% reduction in client-side bundle size, 37% faster First Contentful Paint (FCP),...

item.title: Mastering @RequestHeader in Spring Boot
item.description: Learn how to use @RequestHeader in Spring Boot to handle HTTP headers for authentication, metadata, and client information with practical real-world examples. What is @RequestHeader @RequestHeader...

item.title: Mastering On-Device GenAI: How to Fine-Tune LLMs for Android Using LoRA and Kotlin 2.x
item.description: The dream of a truly personal AI—one that lives entirely on your smartphone, understands your medical history, drafts your legal emails, and critiques your code without ever sending a single byte...

item.title: Everyone is Building MCP-Powered AI Apps Now But Is Model Context Protocol Actually Worth The Hype?
item.description: Every week, another startup says it is shipping an MCP-powered AI app, and honestly, the pitch sounds irresistible: connect your model to tools, data, workflows, and boom, your product gets...

item.title: New Linux 'Copy Fail' Vulnerability Enables Root Access on Major Distributions
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a Linux local privilege escalation (LPE) flaw that could allow an unprivileged local user to obtain root. The high-severity vulnerability...

item.title: PlayStation now requires a ‘one-time online check’ to confirm you own a game
item.description: Sony has finally stepped in to clear up the growing confusion around a new DRM (Digital Rights Management) system on PlayStation 5 and PlayStation 4 consoles. Users reported that the system's...

item.title: Reid Hoffman Thinks Doctors Should Ask AI for a Second Opinion
item.description: The LinkedIn cofounder now has an AI drug discovery startup—and thinks not asking chatbots for medical advice is “bordering on committing malpractice.”

item.title: Google Fixes CVSS 10 Gemini CLI CI RCE and Cursor Flaws Enable Code Execution
item.description: Google has addressed a maximum severity security flaw in Gemini CLI -- the "@google/gemini-cli" npm package and the "google-github-actions/run-gemini-cli" GitHub Actions workflow -- that could...

item.title: SoftBank is creating a robotics company that builds data centers — and already eyeing a $100B IPO
item.description: You need infrastructure to build AI a and robots, but apparently you also need AI and robots to build infrastructure.

item.title: Amazon’s cloud business is surging — and so is its capital spending
item.description: The e-commerce giant is making more money than expected from AWS but it's also spending a lot, and will continue to do so in the near term, its chief executive said.

item.title: Sources: Anthropic could raise a new $50B round at a valuation of $900B
item.description: The maker of Claude has received multiple pre-emptive offers at valuations in the $850 billion to $900 billion range, according to sources familiar with the matter.

item.title: Elon Musk’s worst enemy in court is Elon Musk
item.description: About five hours into Elon Musk's testimony, I typed the following sentence into my notes: "I have never been more sympathetic to Sam Altman in my life." Musk's direct testimony was an improvement...

item.title: On the stand, Elon Musk can’t escape his own tweets
item.description: Elon Musk took the stand for the second day for his attempt to legally dismantle OpenAI.

item.title: Meta is still burning money on AR/VR
item.description: Meta is losing billions on Reality Labs each quarter, and its AI expenditures are only going to increase its spending.

item.title: Satya Nadella says he’s ready to ‘exploit’ the new OpenAI deal
item.description: Microsoft gets to offer OpenAI's tech to its cloud customers and doesn't have to pay for it. "We fully plan to exploit it," Nadella said.

item.title: How Elon Musk Squeezed OpenAI: They 'Are Gonna Want to Kill Me’
item.description: Tensions flared on the third day of trial in Musk v. Altman as OpenAI’s lawyers cross-examined Musk.

item.title: Microsoft says it has over 20M paid Copilot users, and they really are using it
item.description: Despite the lingering perception that no one really uses Copilot, Microsoft said on Wednesday that the number of users and engagement is growing.

item.title: Google Cloud surpasses $20B, but says growth was capacity-constrained
item.description: Google Cloud topped $20B in quarterly revenue for the first time, fueled by surging demand for AI. But capacity constraints mean it could have grown even faster.

item.title: Grindr — yes, Grindr — won the WHCD party circuit
item.description: Hello and welcome to Regulator, a newsletter for Verge subscribers about technology, politics, and technology learning how to politick. If you're not a subscriber but would like to support our...

item.title: Splatoon Raiders preorders for the Switch 2 are nearly 20 percent off
item.description: Nintendo recently announced a new pricing policy, which knocks $10 off the cost of digital versions of future first-party titles exclusive to the Nintendo Switch 2. Splatoon Raiders, for instance,...

item.title: Microsoft reports sinking Xbox revenue as its cloud business climbs
item.description: Microsoft's Xbox hardware revenue continues to tumble, with the company revealing a 33 percent decline as part of its earnings report released on Wednesday. Even though the rest of Microsoft's...

item.title: Google Search queries hit an ‘all time high’ last quarter
item.description: Google Search queries hit an "all time high" in the first quarter of 2026, according to a statement from CEO Sundar Pichai published as part of Alphabet's earnings on Wednesday. "Our AI...

item.title: All the evidence unveiled so far in Musk v. Altman
item.description: The Musk v. Altman trial is underway, and that means exhibits, or the evidence to be presented in court, are being revealed piece by piece. So far, email exchanges, photos, and corporate documents...

item.title: Ubuntu’s AI plans have Linux users looking for a ‘kill switch’
item.description: Canonical's plan to add AI features to Ubuntu has some users asking for "a version of Ubuntu that does not include these features," while others say they'll stick with older versions of the Linux...
